People who trust God for their safety must use appropriate methods, or else they tempt him. God will provide, but we also must. Hezekiah gathered his people together and spoke encouragingly to them. A confident belief in God raises us above the common fear of others. Let the good subjects and soldiers of Jesus Christ rely on his word and boldly say, Since God is for us, who can be against us? By God’s favor, enemies are defeated, and friends are gained.

God left Hezekiah to himself so that, through this trial and his weakness in it, what was in his heart might be known; he was not as perfect in grace as he believed. It is good for us to know ourselves and our own weakness and sinfulness so that we may not be conceited or self-confident, but always live depending on God’s grace. We do not know the corruption of our own hearts, nor what we will do if God leaves us to ourselves. His mistake was that his heart was proud. What need have important people, good people, and useful people to study their own weaknesses and failings and their obligation to free grace so they never think highly of themselves but earnestly ask God always to keep them humble? Hezekiah repaid God poorly for his kindness by making even those kindnesses the source and fuel of his pride. Let us avoid situations that lead to sin; let us avoid company, entertainment, books, yes, even sights that may encourage sin. Let us commit ourselves continually to God’s care and protection and ask him never to leave us or abandon us. Blessed be God, death will soon end the believer's struggle; then pride and every sin will be removed. He will no longer be tempted to withhold praise which belongs to the God of his salvation.
